summary: FL Ceremony M3 backend + frontend tested. 18/19 pytest pass (1 SKIPPED — MockKBA random answers couldn't pass the 5-q quiz; gate logic itself verified by 'deed_without_kba_not_ready' test showing the KBA gate correctly stays false while jurisdiction & AV pass). 1 backend bug found and FIXED: POST /api/fl/ceremony/will/witnesses/invite returned 500 because Mongo's insert_one mutates the dict to include _id ObjectId which then breaks FastAPI JSON encoding. Patched fl_ceremony_routes.py to exclude both 'token_hash' and '_id' from the response. Frontend: /florida landing exposes the new CTA (cta-ceremony-readiness) linking to /florida/ceremony-readiness; the readiness page renders all required sections (readiness-summary, jurisdiction-section, kba-section, av-section), switching document-type to 'will' reveals witnesses-section + invite form; A/V submit with 1280x720/48kHz/60s flips the av-quality gate to passed; invalid /florida/witness/:token shows witness-error card.

minor.issue: storage_service.apply_fl_retention_lock receives S3 AccessDenied (s3:PutObjectRetention not allowed on IAM user). Tag is still recorded in DB ledger with object_lock_applied=false, so the dev fallback works as documented; in production the IAM policy must grant s3:PutObjectRetention for FL_10YR locks to actually apply on the bucket.
Returns 404 — endpoint name in M1 may have changed; verify M1 regression URL path (other /api/fl/compliance/* not enumerated here).

integration_issues.issue: Defaults are fl_nexus_basis='real_estate_in_fl' AND both concerns_fl_property/concerns_fl_law unchecked. Clicking Submit yields backend 400 ('Florida nexus required'). UX should either auto-check concerns_fl_property when basis='real_estate_in_fl' (and concerns_fl_law when basis='fl_law_governed'), or relax the backend rule to accept these bases without the parallel boolean. Toast surfaces the error correctly so it's not silent, but the user has no obvious hint that the checkboxes are needed.

issues: Gate names render as lowercased 'jurisdiction qualifier', 'kba', 'witnesses', 'av quality' (text-transform: capitalize on words from key.replace('_',' ')). 'kba' should display 'KBA' and 'av quality' should display 'A/V quality'. Minor polish item.

action_items: (OPTIONAL) Improve UX on /florida/ceremony-readiness so default form state for jurisdiction qualifier doesn't 400 — auto-derive concerns_fl_property/concerns_fl_law from selected basis, OR show inline hint near the checkboxes.
(OPTIONAL polish) Capitalize KBA / A/V properly in the gate chip labels.

(PROD) Grant s3:PutObjectRetention to notarychain-s3-service IAM user so FL_10YR Object Lock actually applies; currently silently falls back to DB-only ledger which is acceptable per spec but should be tracked.
(NICE-TO-HAVE) Verify /api/fl/compliance/state-profile/FL path (returned 404 in this run — may be /api/fl/compliance/state/FL or similar).
critical_code_review_comments: fl_ceremony_routes.py line 215 (pre-fix) returned the raw witness dict that included Mongo's mutated _id field — fixed by adding '_id' to the exclusion set. Apply the same defensive pattern anywhere a dict is returned right after insert_one().

retention/tag endpoint accepts arbitrary JSON via request.json() instead of a Pydantic model — inconsistent with the rest of the file. Suggest adding a RetentionTagReq(BaseModel) for input validation and openapi schema clarity.

Jurisdiction validation allows fl_nexus_basis='other' only when concerns_fl_property OR concerns_fl_law is true; this is correct but should be documented in the qualifier UI so users selecting 'Other' know they must also tick one box.

Readiness endpoint's KBA fallback ('not-ceremony-linked recent pass within 1h') is reasonable but undocumented — surface this in the gate detail so QA/audit can see WHICH attempt satisfied the gate (already returns attempt_id, good).
FL_NEXUS_BASES is a tuple at module scope but the inline error message recomputes the tuple repr each call — micro-perf nit only.
All /api/fl/ceremony/* endpoints depend on _get_user which itself does an extra users find on every call; consider FastAPI Depends() to reuse auth across endpoints in this router.

success_rate.backend: 100% (18/18 executed; 1 skipped due to mock KBA non-determinism, not a defect)
success_rate.frontend: 100% on the flows tested (landing CTA, witness invalid card, readiness page renders all sections, document-type switch, A/V submit)

test_credentials: demo@test.com / Demo123!  ·  admin@notarychain.com / Admin123!  ·  notary2@test.com / Notary123!
seed_data_creation: Created several test ceremonies (cer-test-*, cer-will-*, cer-w-*, cer-bad-*) under demo@test.com: fl_jurisdiction_qualifications (~3 rows), fl_will_witnesses (2 invited+accepted), fl_av_quality_reports (2 rows), fl_retention_tags (1 row). All safely scoped per-ceremony_id; no shared resources mutated.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend fix already in place (witness invite _id exclusion). pytest /app/backend/tests/test_fl_ceremony_m3.py -v is repeatable; 'test_readiness_after_kba' is intentionally skipped when MockKBA returns passed=false on all-zero answers — to make it deterministic, expose a test-only KBA bypass or have the test login as a user with a precomputed passing attempt. demo user's KBA daily attempts may be near the 2/24h cap from this run plus iteration 97 leftovers, so the skipped test may not even be runnable until the cap window resets.
rca of the issue: Witness invite 500: fl_ceremony_routes.invite_will_witness called db.fl_will_witnesses.insert_one(witness); pymongo/motor mutates the input dict in-place by adding _id (ObjectId). The subsequent dict comprehension only filtered out 'token_hash', so _id leaked into FastAPI's jsonable_encoder which can't serialize ObjectId — surfaces as 'TypeError: ObjectId object is not iterable' wrapped into a 500. Fix: extend the exclusion set to {'token_hash','_id'}. Reproduction: any second consecutive successful invite call (first invite happened to succeed end-to-end pre-fix because email + DB write completed before the encoder ran on the response path — log shows email sent, then ASGI exception). Verified fixed: 18/19 tests pass post-patch including the 2-witness invite scenario and the public token GET/accept loop.
